ai_agent.role: Manages PTO accrual and tracking, sick day balances, absence registration, and workforce availability across all departments

staff.name: Dani Campos
staff.role: HR Lead — PTO management, sick day tracking, absence registration, balance calculations

staff.name: Julia Costa
staff.role: HR Support — assists with tracking, payroll coordination

core_knowledge.description: Accrual every 6 months from start date. Red (Leadership): 5 days per cycle = 10 days/year. Full-time (8h): standard allocation. Part-time (6h): 2 days 1st semester, 3 days 2nd semester = 5 days/year. Special contracts may vary (e.g., Camila has 5 sick days). PTOs are NOT cumulative and expire after 1 year.
Red (Leadership): 4 days. Standard employees: 3 days. Not cumulative. Special contracts may override (e.g., Camila: 5 sick days). Reset annually.

Department leader marks absence in Google Calendar → Dani receives automatic notification (email + Slack) for future/same-day events → CRITICAL: Calendar does NOT notify for past-date entries → Dani registers in absence spreadsheet → Debits PTO only AFTER the day passes (prevents cancellation issues) → Marks green after debit → If PTO exceeds balance: registers as 'falta' (unexcused absence).
During peak construction season, PTO requires 2 weeks advance notice. Leaders must check employee balance before scheduling (currently no visibility tool for leaders).

daily_workflows.name: Daily Absence Monitoring
steps: Check Google Calendar for scheduled absences (today and upcoming)
Check email and Slack for Calendar notifications
Manually check for any past-date entries (Calendar doesn't notify)
Register absences in absence tracking spreadsheet
For past days: debit PTO from employee balance
If PTO exceeds balance: register as 'falta'
critical_decisions: Whether absence is PTO or falta (unexcused)

daily_workflows.name: PTO Accrual Cycle Management
steps: Calculate next accrual date for each employee (every 6 months from start)
Apply correct accrual amount based on category (Red/8h/6h/Special)
Calculate expiration date (1 year from accrual)
Update PTO balance in tracking spreadsheet
Notify employee of new PTO availability

handoffs.trigger: Leader schedules PTO or sick day for team member via Google Calendar
handoffs.data_exchanged: Employee name, absence date(s), absence type
handoffs.impact: Triggers PTO debit or sick day debit

handoffs.data_exchanged: Absence records, falta (unexcused) entries for payroll deduction
handoffs.impact: Payroll accuracy depends on correct absence tracking

pain_points.description: Leaders schedule PTO without checking employee balance — no visible balance tool for leaders
pain_points.current_workaround: Dani manually checks balance when PTO request comes in; sometimes catches after the fact
pain_points.proposed_solution: Self-service PTO balance check tool for leaders before scheduling

pain_points.description: Google Calendar does NOT notify for past-date entries — leaders sometimes enter absences retroactively with no notification to HR
pain_points.current_workaround: Dani manually reviews calendar daily for any backdated entries
pain_points.proposed_solution: Automated alert for any Calendar entry with past date

pain_points.description: Highly manual process — daily calendar monitoring, manual balance calculations, manual expiration tracking
pain_points.proposed_solution: HR module in Const App with automated accrual, debit, and balance tracking

tribal_knowledge.description: Certain employees have negotiated PTO/sick day amounts different from standard policy (e.g., Camila has 5 sick days). These are tracked in the spreadsheet but the contract terms are only known to Dani.
Complex calculation based on individual start dates, 6-month cycles, and 1-year expiration windows. Dani maintains this manually across 72+ employees.
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: Missed accruals or expired PTO not removed from balance

training_curriculum.quiz_coverage: Accrual rates, category rules, Calendar notification gaps, debit timing

topics: How to check employee PTO balance
Advance notice requirements (2 weeks peak season)
training_curriculum.quiz_coverage: Balance checking, advance notice rules, Calendar usage

compliance_requirements.requirement: PTO records maintained for all employees with accurate accrual and debit tracking
Sick day usage documented and tracked per employee category

key_metrics.target: 100% of absences captured and debited correctly
key_metrics.measurement: Monthly audit of Calendar entries vs spreadsheet debits

key_metrics.target: 100% of accruals applied within 1 day of cycle date
<5% of total absences classified as falta
key_metrics.measurement: Monthly count of faltas vs total absences
